items.title: Will funding follow the kudos?
items.summary: During a House Appropriations subcommittee hearing on Wednesday, Democrats and Republicans lauded community colleges for their critical role in preparing students for in-demand jobs. items.title: Expanding credit for prior learning
items.summary: Turning prior learning, work experience, military service and other credentials into college credit can save students time and money. Despite that benefit, it’s been underutilized. Only about 11% of adult learners, for example, have benefited... The post Expanding credit for prior learning first appeared on Community College Daily .

linkedin_drafts.title: MA budget watch: SUCCESS Fund signals
linkedin_drafts.text:
Massachusetts’ Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) is one of the most concrete examples of policy translating into student-support capacity—peer mentors, skills workshops, transfer-related activities, and targeted advising.

Anchor link I check during budget season: MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/)

Question for colleagues: which SUCCESS-funded intervention has produced the clearest retention/completion gains on your campus?

#CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #HigherEdPolicy #Massachusetts

linkedin_drafts.title: Federal policy watch: what community colleges can act on
linkedin_drafts.text:
Federal policy and funding signals matter most when they change what colleges can *do* this semester: aid eligibility, workforce grants, reporting requirements, or program approval.

One item on my radar: U.S. Department of Labor RSS: releases.xml (https://www.dol.gov/rss/releases.xml)

If you’re in advising or student success, what’s the most helpful way you’ve communicated major policy shifts to students—FAQs, short videos, office hours, or targeted messaging?

#HigherEdPolicy #CommunityColleges #FinancialAid #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising

linkedin_drafts.title: AI in higher ed: governance beats hype
linkedin_drafts.text:
AI adoption in higher education is increasingly a governance challenge: approved use cases, human oversight, privacy/procurement, and how we measure impact.

One item I’m tracking: WIOA revamp needed to boost employer-led training (https://www.ccdaily.com/2026/03/wiao-revap-needed-to-boost-employer-led-training/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=wiao-revap-needed-to-boost-employer-led-training)

For student support/advising: what guardrails have worked well at your institution?

#AIinEducation #HigherEdLeadership #AcademicAdvising #StudentSuccess #EdTech
